input.headline: Moment a Gustav Klimt painting sells for record amount

raw.headline: Moment a Gustav Klimt painting sells for record amount

winner: Why did Gustav Klimt decide to become a weather forecaster? Because his paintings consistently break sales records when there's a gustav!

candidates.text: Why did the Gustav Klimt painting refuse to attend the art auction? It said, "I'm already framed!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'framed' (both literally and in context of being caught in a situation) are clever and specific to the painting's context."}

candidates.text: A Gustav Klimt painting just sold for an astonishing sum. I guess they really meant it when they said "priceless.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific to the context of art sales. It uses a play on words with 'priceless' in a clever way."}

candidates.text: Why did Gustav Klimt decide to become a weather forecaster? Because his paintings consistently break sales records when there's a gustav!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with the pun on 'gustav' and 'Gustav Klimt,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity in connecting the weather forecaster angle to his actual work."}

candidates.text: Why did the Gustav Klimt painting refuse to join the art party? Because it didn't want to be outshone at the record sale-tea!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the play on 'sale-tea,' but it feels forced and lacks natural flow. The reference to the record sale is present, but the humor doesn't fully land due to awkward phrasing and limited surprise."}

candidates.text: Why did Gustav Klimt's painting refuse to go to school? Because it had already sold for a record amount.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat forced and relies on a pun that doesn't fully connect to the headline. It's brief and has some surprise, but lacks specificity and natural flow."}
